Member visited 19 unique providers and 8 unique pharmacies in the past 90 days. 11 controlled substance prescriptions filled during this period. Pattern concentrated in Southeast MN area. Estimated exposure $734,575. Initiating member utilization review.

AUTHOR: Jennifer Olson
NOTE_TEXT: Field observation of Dr. Steven Anderson's clinic over 3 business days. Patient volume count shows average of 15 patients per day entering the facility, consistent with appointment schedule. However, billed volume for those dates shows 32 patient encounters - a discrepancy of 13 phantom visits per day. Photographic evidence and time-stamped logs maintained.

AUTHOR: David Nakamura
NOTE_TEXT: Reviewed 25 claims flagged for duplicate billing. Confirmed 29 true duplicates where identical services were billed and paid on the same date of service for the same patient. Additional 9 claims appear to be legitimate corrections or adjustments. Net confirmed overpayment: $740,548.

AUTHOR: David Nakamura
NOTE_TEXT: Algorithm detection flagged Dr. Joshua Patel for billing anomalies. Analysis shows 9 duplicate claims submitted over the past 6 months totaling $447,852. AUTHOR: Rebecca Johnson
NOTE_TEXT: Alert generated for Pine City Pharmacy showing controlled substance dispensing concentrated from only 18 prescribers accounting for 91% of all Schedule II fills. Pattern consistent with pill mill operation indicators. Total Schedule II dispensing volume is 20x above peer benchmark. Initiating pharmacy investigation.

AUTHOR: Angela Peterson
NOTE_TEXT: Conducted on-site interview with Dr. Anthony Olson regarding billing patterns. Provider stated that higher E/M coding reflects complexity of patient population. However, review of 15 randomly selected charts showed only 4 of 15 (27%) had documentation supporting the billed level. Provider became defensive when shown peer comparison data. Provider's office manager acknowledged issues with coding staff training.

AUTHOR: Michael Swanson
NOTE_TEXT: Completed review of 50 medical records from Mark Jackson's practice. Findings: 34 of 50 encounters (68%) were upcoded by at least one E/M level. 12 encounters had minimal documentation - brief notes that would support 99212 at most but were billed as 99215. Total overbilling for sampled claims is $350,418. Extrapolating to full claim volume suggests exposure of $1,067,552.

AUTHOR: Robert Kim
NOTE_TEXT: Conducted unannounced field visit to Summit Pharmacy St Paul. Observations: small storefront with minimal OTC inventory typical of dispensing-focused operation. During 2-hour observation, 6 patients arrived in groups of 2-3, all presenting prescriptions from Dr. Donald Miller. Multiple patients appeared to know each other. Pharmacy staff processed prescriptions rapidly with minimal patient counseling. Photographs and detailed notes filed.
